title: Packages, Repositories, Dependencies, and Software Provenance · System Lens: System Project Dependencies
summary: Install or update a required tool while preserving repository trust, version evidence, rollback options, and the boundary between system and project dependencies. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux system project dependencies increment in a distribution-aware package change record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate distribution packages, language/project environments, containers, and manually installed binaries by ownership and update path.
Evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict System Project Dependencies
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host proposes lab-tool 5.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 5.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the System Project Dependencies contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate distribution packages, language/project environments, containers, and manually installed binaries by ownership and update path.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to System Project Dependencies while producing a distribution-aware package change record.

content.title: Non-worked case
